title: Bhuteshwar Temple
tagline: Bhuteshwar Temple — spiritual town of Haryana

overview.short: Bhuteshwar Temple is a Hindu temple in Jind, Haryana, in the series of 48 kos parikrama of Kurukshetra, dedicated to Bhuteshwar, a manifestation of Shiva.
overview.description: Bhuteshwar Temple is a Hindu temple in Jind, Haryana, in the series of 48 kos parikrama of Kurukshetra, dedicated to Bhuteshwar, a manifestation of Shiva. It was built by Raghbir Singh. Because it has a large water tank all around it is also called Rani Talab (meaning Queen Pond) .

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Jind
topPlaces.description: Jind is one of the largest and oldest cities in Jind district in the Indian state of Haryana. It is the administrative headquarter of Jind district.

topPlaces.name: Bir Bara Ban Wildlife Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Bir Bara Ban Wildlife Sanctuary is situated 5 km away from Jind on Jind-hansi Road in Haryana State, India. It is spread over an area of 419.26 hectares.

topPlaces.name: Jamni
topPlaces.description: Jamni is a village in Pillukhera tehsil in Jind district of Haryana State, India. It belongs to the Hisar division.

topPlaces.name: Barodi, Jind
topPlaces.description: Barodi is a village in Jind sub-district of Jind District in Haryana, India.

items.note: Jind is one of the largest and oldest cities in Jind district in the Indian state of Haryana. It is the administrative headquarter of Jind district.
Bir Bara Ban Wildlife Sanctuary is situated 5 km away from Jind on Jind-hansi Road in Haryana State, India. It is spread over an area of 419.26 hectares.
Jamni is a village in Pillukhera tehsil in Jind district of Haryana State, India. It belongs to the Hisar division.
Barodi is a village in Jind sub-district of Jind District in Haryana, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bhuteshwar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bhuteshwar Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bhuteshwar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Indira Gandhi International Airport, Delhi (114 km) and the nearest railway station is New Delhi Railway Station (115 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bhuteshwar Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bhuteshwar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bhuteshwar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Bhuteshwar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bhuteshwar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Jind, Bir Bara Ban Wildlife Sanctuary, Jamni, Barodi, Jind.
